name: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter
fact: MRO's HiRISE camera resolves objects ≥30 cm on the Martian surface — sharp enough to find Beagle 2's wreckage in 2015 and to image Curiosity and Perseverance from orbit. Acts as the primary high-bandwidth relay for surface assets.
capability: Highest-resolution orbital imaging of any planet; the eyes-in-the-sky for every recent Mars surface mission.
